title: Nested Blocks of Code Should Not Be Left Empty
description: An empty code block can be confusing. It might be unclear whether it's left empty intentionally, or if it indicates that the implementation is incomplete. This rule checks for empty nested blocks of code in Java files.
recommendation: Remove or fill the empty code blocks to eliminate ambiguity and maintain straightforward, less surprising code.
impact: Leaving empty blocks of code can lead to confusion and potential bugs in the software due to misunderstanding if the block is empty intentionally or not.

noncompliant: for (int i = 0; i < 42; i++){}  // Noncompliant: is the block empty on purpose, or is code missing?

checks.message: Remove or fill the empty code blocks to eliminate ambiguity and maintain straightforward, less surprising code.
